antipattern.symptom: Devs absorb 'small' adds during sprint; budget overruns by 30% with no scope decision recorded.
antipattern.root-cause: No change-control gate; absorbed changes treated as quality of life.
antipattern.fix: Every add >2h goes through change-control with impact analysis.

antipattern.symptom: AC 'looks good' or 'meets expectations'; handoff disputes block release.
antipattern.root-cause: AC written in business-prose, not testable form.
antipattern.fix: Rewrite AC in Gherkin form; reject untestable AC at baseline.

antipattern.symptom: RTM created at kickoff and never updated; orphans grow.
antipattern.root-cause: No owner; coverage check not in CI.
antipattern.fix: Assign RTM owner; rtm_coverage.py in weekly CI.

antipattern.symptom: Stakeholders later claim 'I assumed X was included'.
antipattern.fix: Force at least one exclusion; review adjacent expectations explicitly.
